mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because disinflation preserves room for easier policy.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal disappears when CPIAUCSL is flat-to-down and Rank IC stays below 0.03 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income expectations, and lower equity earnings outlooks, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the average forward return after a 0.3pp/3m UNRATE rise is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A broad contraction in retail sales and housing starts indicates weakening final demand, lower revenue growth, and weaker risk appetite, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not stronger than either series alone across rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO implies improving industrial output and corporate operating leverage, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be clearest when payroll growth is also positive.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO episodes fail to coincide with above-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction with PAYEMS does not improve predictive power relative to INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration after a prior cooldown raises expected policy restraint and compresses valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect should be strongest when FEDFUNDS is already low, because markets reprice the path of future hikes more aggressively.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration does not precede weak 5-day SPX returns, or if the signal remains negative even when FEDFUNDS changes are absent and inflation reacceleration is not accompanied by policy repricing.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 relative to prior quarters signals renewed aggregate demand and higher forward earnings capacity, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal should fail when the GDP revision risk is large enough to reverse the growth interpretation.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth does not correspond to above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship is unstable across revised and unrevised GDP vintages.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor income momentum deteriorates and consumer spending expectations weaken, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is not active if unemployment rises without payroll deceleration.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth turns negative while UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-slowdown and unemployment-rise condition does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in RSAFS and HOUST marks broadening household and construction demand weakness, which reduces cyclical earnings expectations and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. This short-horizon effect should be strongest in a tightening cycle.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both fall below their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in RSAFS and HOUST does not predict negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes outside tightening cycles and does not outperform either series separately.
